rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ALLURA FONG SPECIAL NEEDS TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #15, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/15/26. Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) There were 329.34 shares of Franklin Templeton on hand at the beginning of this accounting period. 44.276 shares were reinvested, which should total 373.616. (2) If the Fidelity Equity Dividend Income mutual fund was redeemed for a higher value than the value of the 2,590.851 shares on hand at the beginning of this accounting period ($65,140.85) plus the 9.867 shares reinvested on 4/5/24 ($282.40), why does Schedule F show the CV of the 2,600.72 shares as $72,924.13? What does the additional redemption of $5,864.83 represent? The Schedule of Gains (Losses) (which is it?) shows the proceeds from the redemption (without showing the number of shares) as $66,777.00. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 7/6/26. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (A supplement or amendment to a petition should simply provide the missing or different information, not in "response" to issues raised by an examiner. [CRC 7.3.]) Additional bond or missing bank statements, etc., should just be filed; there is no need to state that it or they will be filed.)

rulings.Case Title: SARA R. JULIAN IRREVOCABLE TRUST CREATED FOR THE BENEFIT OF ISAAC JULIAN BOOTS,DATED 1/16/18 EST. PURSUANT TO COURT
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #4, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/3/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Did the trustee (who is also the conservator of the settlor's estate) file his updated proposed hourly fee schedule? [Probate Code Section 2614.8.] This was a significant increase beginning in March 2024. (2) Why was the Morgan Stanley account number changed from xxx1749 to xxx1713 and where were the changes in the number of shares of the funds shown? Schedule G does not appear to support the difference between the number of shares on hand in xxx1749 as of 2/29/24 and the number of shares on hand in xxx1713 at the end of this accounting period. (3) The market value of the funds in xxx1713 shown on Schedule E also do not agree with the market value shown in the statement. (4) Why was the Sara Julian SNT paid a bond premium (?) on 4/25/24? (5) Under what authority was Kate Julian paid (reimbursed?) for "education" costs that included travel and meals? Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 7/20/26. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (The order signed on 8/7/23 but FILED on 8/8/23 that permitted the increase in monthly support and payment for therapy was filed in THIS case, which is the irrevocable trust fbo Isaac Julian Boots. Paragraph 5 refers to "Ms. Julian" having annual income; this trust is for her son.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOHN A. MOKRICKY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Determination Of Entitlement To Estate Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The attorney's update (filed 6/24/26, not verified by the petitioner) says that Richard A. McKee acted as the personal representative and only beneficiary of Robert J. McKee's estate in the New Mexico probate case. But he was already discharged as the personal rep. The supplement includes a copy of the court's final order for distributing the estate but does not include the schedule of distribution. It is not clear if the order covers the interest in this estate or has an omnibus clause for assets found later. The Court will determine whether the information on record is sufficient. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JING ZHONG DENG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 07/27/2026. Issues necessitating continuance: 1) No notice of hearing with completed proof of service has been filed with the court (Probate Code Section 13655). 2) Petition is inconsistent. Item 5a(1) is marked, indicating that decedent was survived by no child. However, attachment 9 lists grandchildren, suggesting that decedent was survived either by a child or by issue of a predeceased child. In a supplemental declaration verified by petitioner, please clarify the names and dates of death of decedent's children, and the names of their surviving issue. 3) Paragraph 14 of attachment 7 indicates that declarations from the grandchildren in support of the petition will be filed, but no such declarations have been filed. 4) The grant deed attached shows ownership of the real property as "Jun Deng and Janet Xiao-Hong Chen, husband and wife, as community property, and Jing Zhong Deng and Mavis Deng, husband and wife, as community property, all as joint tenants." In a supplemental declaration verified by petitioner, please indicate whether Jing Zhong Deng and Mavis Deng survived Jun Deng And Janet Xiao-Hong Chen. 5) No proposed order has been received or lodged with the court (LRSF 14.7.A). Please address the above issues and deliver courtesy copies of all further filings to this examiner no later than 07/13/2026 to avoid additional continuance.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RALPH JENSEN RACHAL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Procedural issues with the petition: 1) No proof of publication has been filed (Probate Code Section 8120 and 8121). 2) No Notice of Petition to Administer Estate (Form DE-121) has been filed (Probate Code Section 8100). All notices must include language about remote appearances (LRSF 14.1.C.6). 3) Form DE-147S must be separately filed with a cover page, not attached to the Form DE-147 (LRSF 14.18.E). Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.
